title: Get your acceptance letter
description: Receive the official acceptance letter from UwS International Office via email. Print a copy to bring with you.

title: Get your EHIC card
description: Apply for a European Health Insurance Card from your home country's health authority. This covers emergency medical care in Poland.

title: Arrange travel insurance
description: Get travel/health insurance that covers your entire stay. Some universities require this in addition to EHIC.

title: Book transport to Siedlce
description: Fly to Warsaw Chopin (WAW) or Modlin (WMI) airport. From Warsaw, take a direct PKP train to Siedlce (~1.5 hours). Buy tickets at intercity.pl.

title: Pack essential documents
description: Passport/ID, acceptance letter, EHIC card, insurance documents, passport photos (4 pcs), Learning Agreement, bank statements.

title: Check in to dormitory
description: Go to DS5 reception with your passport and acceptance letter. You will get your room key and dormitory rules.

title: Visit the International Office
description: Go to the International Relations Office (Rektorat building) to register your arrival. Bring passport, acceptance letter, and photos.

title: Get your student ID card
description: The International Office will arrange your student ID (legitymacja). This gives you discounts on public transport (50%) and cultural venues.

title: Register for PESEL number
description: Visit the Siedlce City Hall (Urząd Miasta) to register your stay and get a PESEL number. Needed for bank accounts and official matters. Bring your passport.

title: Open a Polish bank account
description: Go to PKO BP, mBank, or Santander with your passport and PESEL. A Polish account avoids international transfer fees. Many students use Revolut as an alternative.

title: Buy a Polish SIM card
description: Get a prepaid SIM from Orange, Play, Plus, or T-Mobile at any kiosk or electronics store. ~25-30 PLN/month for calls + data. Registration requires passport.

title: Get a public transport card
description: Buy a monthly bus pass at the MZK office or kiosk. With student ID, you get a 50% discount. Monthly pass costs ~25 PLN (student rate).

title: Learn the recycling system
description: Poland uses color-coded bins: Yellow (plastic/metal), Blue (paper), Green (glass), Brown (bio), Black (mixed). Dormitories have designated collection points.

title: Save emergency contacts
description: Emergency: 112 | Police: 997 | Ambulance: 999 | Fire: 998 | UwS International Office: +48 25 643 19 21 | Siedlce Hospital: +48 25 640 61 00

title: Finalize your Learning Agreement
description: Meet your academic coordinator at UwS to confirm or modify your course selection. Sign the Learning Agreement and send a copy to your home university.

title: Attend orientation events
description: Join the Erasmus orientation organized by the International Office. Great way to meet other exchange students and learn about the city.

title: Plan a Warsaw day trip
description: Warsaw is only 1.5 hours by train. Visit the Old Town, Lazienki Park, and POLIN Museum. Weekend return tickets are cheaper.
